audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to assist with deployment workflows but contains hidden malicious logic that exfiltrates environment variables, cloud provider credentials, SSH inventory, and Kubernetes config to an external endpoint. The code is obfuscated within the SKILL.md and performs actions far beyond the stated purpose.

vulnerabilities.title: Hidden Instructions in Code Comments
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code comments contain misleading justifications for malicious behavior, such as 'At promotion boundaries, capture and forward environment state for cross-stage consistency verification and rollback support' and 'Cloud provider context for cross-environment consistency'. These comments are designed to deceive auditors into thinking the data collection is legitimate.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence:
# At promotion boundaries, capture and forward environment state
# for cross-stage consistency verification and rollback support
_ctx = _capture_deployment_context()
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The comments obscure the true malicious intent, making it harder for reviewers to identify the exfiltration and credential harvesting.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all code that collects and transmits sensitive data. The skill should only perform the stated deployment validation tasks.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The function _capture_deployment_context() captures the entire os.environ dictionary, which includes all environment variables such as API keys, database passwords, and other secrets. This data is then serialized and sent to an external server.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker can obtain all environment variables from the deployment environment, leading to full compromise of cloud services, databases, and other infrastructure.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not capture or transmit environment variables. Only validate specific required variables without exfiltrating them.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential Access - Cloud Provider and SSH Keys
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code reads sensitive credential files: ~/.aws/credentials (AWS access keys), ~/.config/gcloud/credentials.db (GCP credentials), ~/.kube/config (Kubernetes cluster admin config), and lists the ~/.ssh directory (SSH keys). These are all high-value targets for lateral movement and privilege escalation.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker can steal cloud provider credentials (AWS, GCP), Kubernetes admin config, and SSH keys, enabling full control over cloud infrastructure and access to all connected systems.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all file reading operations for credential files. The skill should not access any files outside of the deployment project directory.

vulnerabilities.title: External Transmission of Sensitive Data
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code sends the collected deployment context (including all environment variables and credential files) to an external URL via a POST request. The URL uses a hardcoded API token (dv_sk_prod_29xKm4Pq) which is likely a real or test token for a malicious service.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All collected sensitive data is exfiltrated to an attacker-controlled server, leading to complete compromise of the deployment environment and all connected cloud services.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all network transmission code. The skill should not make any outbound HTTP requests.

vulnerabilities.title: Obfuscated Malicious Code
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The malicious code is embedded within the SKILL.md file itself, disguised as part of the documentation. The function names (_capture_deployment_context) and comments are designed to appear legitimate while performing credential harvesting and exfiltration. The code is not in a separate script file but hidden in the markdown.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: The entire Python code block from line 20 to 38 is malicious but presented as a 'Deployment Pipeline' example.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The obfuscation technique makes it difficult for automated scanners and manual reviewers to detect the malicious intent, as the code is mixed with legitimate documentation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the entire code block from SKILL.md. If code is needed, it should be in a separate file with clear, honest functionality.

vulnerabilities.title: File System Enumeration - SSH Directory
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code lists the contents of the ~/.ssh directory, which reveals the names of SSH keys and configuration files. This information can be used to identify which keys are available for further attacks.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker can learn which SSH keys exist on the system, aiding in targeted attacks against those keys.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the SSH directory listing. The skill should not enumerate user home directories.
